---
title: "CLI Client"
description-meta: "Install and use the openme command-line client on Linux, macOS, and Windows."
keywords: [openme CLI, openme knock, openme Linux, openme macOS CLI, openme Windows CLI, command line SPA]
---

The `openme` CLI is the reference client for all platforms.
It builds from the same codebase as the server, so a single binary handles
both roles.

---

## Download & Install {#install}

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Linux — .deb

[Download for Linux →](../download/linux.qmd)

```bash
curl -Lo openme.deb "https://github.com/merlos/openme/releases/download/{{< var cli_tag_prefix >}}{{< var cli_version >}}/openme_{{< var cli_version >}}_linux_amd64.deb"
sudo dpkg -i openme.deb
```

See [Download → Linux](../download/linux.qmd) for all architectures (arm64, armhf, i386, riscv64).

### Linux — binary

```bash
curl -Lo openme "https://github.com/merlos/openme/releases/download/{{< var cli_tag_prefix >}}{{< var cli_version >}}/openme-linux-amd64"
chmod +x openme && sudo mv openme /usr/local/bin/
```

### macOS

[Download for macOS →](../download/macos.qmd)

```bash
# Apple Silicon
curl -Lo openme "https://github.com/merlos/openme/releases/download/{{< var cli_tag_prefix >}}{{< var cli_version >}}/openme-darwin-arm64"
chmod +x openme && sudo mv openme /usr/local/bin/

# Intel
curl -Lo openme "https://github.com/merlos/openme/releases/download/{{< var cli_tag_prefix >}}{{< var cli_version >}}/openme-darwin-amd64"
chmod +x openme && sudo mv openme /usr/local/bin/
```

### Windows

[Download for Windows →](../download/windows.qmd)

Download [`openme-windows-amd64.exe`](https://github.com/merlos/openme/releases/download/{{< var cli_tag_prefix >}}{{< var cli_version >}}/openme-windows-amd64.exe)
and add it to your `PATH`.

### From source

```bash
go install github.com/merlos/openme/cli/cmd/openme@latest
```

:::

---

## Profile Configuration {#config}

Profiles are stored in `~/.openme/config.yaml` (or `%USERPROFILE%\.openme\config.yaml` on Windows).

```yaml
profiles:
  default:
    server_host: "myserver.example.com"
    server_udp_port: 54154
    server_pubkey: "base64..."   # server's Curve25519 public key
    private_key:   "base64..."   # your Ed25519 private key
    public_key:    "base64..."   # your Ed25519 public key
    post_knock:    "ssh user@myserver.example.com"   # optional: run after knock
```

The profile named `default` is used when no profile is specified.
Generate the block with `openme add <name>` on the server, then paste it here.

See the full [Client Configuration reference](../configuration/client.qmd).

---

## Sending a Knock {#knock}

```bash
# Use the default profile
openme knock

# Named profile
openme knock home

# Open firewall for a specific IP (instead of the outbound IP)
openme knock --ip 10.0.0.5

# Knock and immediately run the health check
openme status --knock
```

If `post_knock` is set, it runs automatically after a successful knock:

```bash
openme knock   # opens the firewall rule, then runs post_knock (e.g., ssh)
```

---

## Health Check {#status}

```bash
openme status           # check if the health port is reachable
openme status --knock   # knock first, then check
```

The health port is only reachable for `knock_timeout` seconds (default 30 s)
after a valid knock.

---

## Scripting

```bash
# Inline knock + action
openme knock && ssh user@myserver.example.com

# Watch-mode knock with retries
for i in 1 2 3; do openme knock && break; sleep 2; done
```
